symptoms.symptom_name: Photosensitive malar rash with symmetric synovitis and fatigue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A UV-triggered malar rash sparing the folds with symmetric small-joint synovitis is a classic SLE flare.
A bilateral symmetric fold-sparing rash is not the pattern of unilateral spreading cellulitis.
Fever in an immunomodulated patient must prompt infection exclusion before immunosuppression.
Low-grade fever can accompany an SLE flare but does not exclude infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: No single hot/red joint; no high fever/rigors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetric involvement without a single fiery joint or rigors argues against septic arthritis, though examination is needed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No pleuritic chest pain/breathlessness or renal symptoms (serositis/nephritis screen)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of pleuritic pain/breathlessness and renal symptoms lowers the probability of serious organ involvement now, but bloods/urine are needed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral symmetric fold-sparing facial rash without unilateral spread/heat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A bilateral fold-sparing rash without unilateral spread/high fever argues against facial cellulitis.
UV is a classic SLE flare trigger.

symptoms.symptom_name: Malar rash sparing folds; symmetric synovitis + knee effusions; clear chest/no rub (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The classic malar rash with symmetric synovitis and a clear chest fits an SLE flare.
No heliotrope rash/proximal weakness argues against dermatomyositis.
Established SLE is the substrate for the flare.
Immunomodulation modestly raises infection risk and lowers the threshold to exclude it.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A photosensitive malar rash with symmetric synovitis and no single hot joint or unilateral spreading facial swelling favours an SLE flare, but a low-grade fever in an immunomodulated patient mandates infection exclusion before steroids, and serious SLE complications (serositis/nephritis) plus the joints/rash cannot be examined remotely — so urgent in-person review with bloods/urine is required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person review with bloods (FBC, CRP, ESR, U&E, complement C3/C4, dsDNA) and urinalysis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To assess the flare, exclude infection, and screen for lupus nephritis; cannot be done via chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Joint aspiration if any single joint becomes disproportionately inflamed
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude septic arthritis if the exam raises concern.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Chest assessment/imaging if pleuritic/respiratory features develop
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To assess serositis if features appear.
